signature: Reachability/decidability for iterated one-dimensional integer maps whose branch is chosen by a local test, plus the "acceleration" toolbox that computes exact closed-form reachability sets of affine loops. The tell I hunted: a community that treats T(n) as "the simplest 1-D piecewise-affine map / one-register affine machine," proves partial decidability by the SHAPE of the region partition (interval/order vs congruence/residue) and by the multiplicative structure of slopes (p-adic weights, powers of 2 and 3), and has a systematic machine — flattening + Presburger/S-unit closure — for exactly the finite-alternation sub-dynamics that our m-cycle equations live in. The load-bearing discriminator across every find: DOES the decidability hinge on the pieces being real intervals (order topology) or residue classes (2-adic topology)? Collatz always sits on the residue side, and that single fact predicts where each theorem does and does not reach.

finds.ref: T. Karimov, F. Luca, J. Nieuwveld, J. Ouaknine, J. Worrell, "On the Decidability of Presburger Arithmetic Expanded with Powers," arXiv:2407.05191 (v2, 2025); SODA 2025.
finds.the_theorem: Thm 1: for ANY integers a,b>1, the EXISTENTIAL fragment of the first-order theory of <Z;0,1,<,+,a^N,b^N> is decidable (a^N = {a^n}). Proof engine = Baker's theorem on linear forms in logarithms (Matveev's effective version, their Thm 3) + an effectiveness result on systems Cz=d over the power-sets. Sharp boundary: full FO with two multiplicatively-independent power predicates is undecidable (Hieronymi-Schulz), and they push undecidability down to just 2 quantifier-alternation blocks; the existential fragment of <N;+,<,x->a^x,x->b^x> being decidable would itself imply breakthroughs on normality of log_3(2).
finds.the_match: Collatz cycle equations ARE existential formulas in <Z;+,<,2^N,3^N>. A parity itinerary with k odd-steps over total length L forces n*(2^L - 3^k) = SUM_{i} 3^{k-1-i} 2^{s_i} (the Steiner/Simons-de Weger m-cycle identity). Fixing the block-shape, "there exists a positive integer on a cycle of this shape" is a single existential sentence with power-terms 2^L, 3^k => DECIDABLE by Thm 1. The match is tight at FIXED shape. The gap: the FULL m-cycle problem quantifies over all shapes/lengths, which crosses into the undecidable two-alternation region — precisely mirroring that Steiner (1-cycles) and Simons-de Weger (m-cycles up to m=91, Hercher 2023) prove exclusions only for BOUNDED cycle structure. Their tool (Baker linear forms in logs) is LITERALLY the tool Steiner and Simons-de Weger use, so the two literatures are computing the same objects unknowingly.
finds.transfer: State and run the KLNOW existential-decision procedure on the accelerated-Collatz k-block cycle sentence phi_k := (exists n,L,s_1..s_k) [ n>0, n*2^L = 3^k*n + C(s_1..s_k), block-ordering constraints ] in <Z;+,<,2^N,3^N>, and verify (i) it is a legal existential sentence, (ii) the decision procedure's Baker bound reproduces Steiner's explicit bound for k=1 and Simons-de Weger's for general fixed k. Conjecture to prove: the m-cycle exclusion frontier coincides exactly with the existential/2-alternation decidability frontier of powers-augmented Presburger.

finds.ref: J. Leroux, G. Sutre, "Flat Counter Automata Almost Everywhere!," ATVA 2005, LNCS 3707, pp. 489-503 (HAL hal-00346310).
finds.the_theorem: Thm 3.4: accelerated symbolic reachability of a counter automaton terminates on (S,I) iff (S,I) is FLAT (control graph reducible to one with no nested loops). Cor 4.4 + Thm 4.3 [CJ98,FL02,Boi03]: for any semilinear path scheme (SLPS) rho, the reachability subrelation R_S(rho) is effectively SEMILINEAR (Presburger-definable); hence post*/pre* of any flat counter machine is effectively Presburger. Prop 4.8: reversal-bounded => flat. So the entire FAST/LASH/TReX acceleration technology computes EXACT Presburger reachability sets for flat (bounded-alternation) sub-dynamics.
finds.the_match: "Orbits with <= k parity blocks" of accelerated Collatz = a FLAT sub-automaton: a run of the parity transducer with <=k alternations is a bounded, nesting-free path scheme. By Cor 4.4 the set {n : orbit prefix realizes block-pattern w} would be Presburger-computable — directly generalizing the 1-cycle/m-cycle equations, which are the periodic special case. THE GAP, sharply: Collatz loop bodies are affine with MULTIPLICATIVE factor 3 (x -> (3x+1)/2), and the matrix monoid {3^i} is INFINITE, so Boigelot's finite-monoid Presburger-closure (the hypothesis behind Thm 4.3 for affine updates) FAILS — the single-loop closure is exactly the non-Presburger set 2^N/3^N. Flatness thus buys the combinatorial reduction to finitely many block-patterns but NOT the per-pattern closure.
finds.transfer: Compose the two toolboxes: use Leroux-Sutre flattening to reduce k-parity-block Collatz reachability to finitely many path schemes, then discharge EACH scheme's closure not in plain Presburger but in the powers-augmented existential theory <Z;+,<,2^N,3^N> (KLNOW). Prove: for every fixed k, k-block Collatz reachability/cycle-existence is decidable via (flatten => finite pattern set => existential powers-Presburger query per pattern). This is the exact point where 'why Collatz escapes acceptance' becomes 'finite monoid fails, powers-of-2/3 extension needed'.

finds.ref: A. M. Ben-Amram, "Mortality of Iterated Piecewise Affine Functions over the Integers: Decidability and Complexity," STACS 2013, LIPIcs 20, 514-525 (journal: Computability 4(1):19-56, 2015).
finds.the_theorem: For integer-valued iterated piecewise-affine functions (regions = convex rational polyhedra; in 1-D just a finite partition of Z into INTERVALS): mortality (every trajectory hits 0) is PSPACE-COMPLETE in dimension 1, PTIME for globally affine maps any dimension, and Pi^0_2-COMPLETE from dimension 2 onward — even with an a-priori bounded number of regions. Undecidability is proved by simulating generalized Collatz functions (division region D that implements x mod m via repeated subtraction along an added coordinate).
finds.the_match: This is the cleanest structural statement of WHY Collatz is hard, in machine-independent terms. The Collatz map's pieces are chosen by CONGRUENCE (parity = 2-adic ball), not by an INTERVAL (order). Ben-Amram's decidable 1-D case requires INTERVAL pieces (his own text: 'in dimension one the regions are just a partition of Z into intervals'); to realize a mod-m branch he must add a second coordinate, landing in the Pi^0_2 regime. Our Space Needle object (value together with v2-valuation dynamics, hitting an exact power of 2) is PRECISELY a 2-D integer piecewise-affine iteration with the valuation as the second coordinate => it sits at Ben-Amram's Pi^0_2-complete level, formally certifying it as a 'cryptid' (no decision procedure expected). Gap: mortality target is 0 vs Collatz's 1 (cosmetic); the exact polyhedral encoding of v2 as a coordinate needs to be written out.
finds.transfer: Formalize Space-Needle-type value+valuation iteration as a concrete 2-region 2-D integer PAF and locate it inside Ben-Amram's Pi^0_2-completeness (giving a rigorous 'undecidable as stated' status), while separately proving the 1-D value-only map is NOT an interval-PAF (order vs 2-adic partition), which explains cleanly why the PSPACE-decidable 1-D fragment cannot swallow Collatz. Deliverable: a theorem 'Collatz-type reachability is interval-PAF-decidable iff the parity partition can be replaced by an order partition — which it cannot.'

finds.ref: O. Kurganskyy, I. Potapov, "Reachability problems for PAMs," arXiv:1510.04121 (2015); journal w/ O. Bournez as "Reachability Problems for One-Dimensional Piecewise Affine Maps," Int. J. Found. Comput. Sci. 29(4):529-549, 2018.
finds.the_theorem: Thm 1: reachability decidable for an injective 2-interval PAM on S^1 admitting an f-invariant density phi bounded 0<K_min<phi<K_max. Thm 2 (p-ADIC WEIGHT condition): reachability decidable for a PAM f whenever, forming the matrix A_f of p-adic norms ||a_i||_p of the slopes, every ROW (fixed prime, across the maps) has entries of one sign (a_{ji}*a_{j'i} >= 0). Cor 2: complete 2-interval PAMs are decidable. Technique = p-adic norms/weights + rational-base (beta-expansion) correspondence.
finds.the_match: Thm 2's hypothesis is testable on accelerated Collatz slopes {1/2, 3/2}: prime 2 sits in the denominator of BOTH (weights same sign); prime 3 sits in the numerator of 3/2 only (positive, the other zero) — so the sign condition a_{ji}*a_{j'i}>=0 is SATISFIED. So the p-adic-weight decidability class is, on the slope data alone, the right kind of class for Collatz. The gap that kills a direct application: KP require a genuine INTERVAL PAM on S^1 (order topology, rational endpoints); embedding Collatz needs the parity split = a 2-adic clopen ball, which is not a real interval, and the induced map is neither injective nor complete. So this is the same order-vs-2-adic obstruction as Ben-Amram, but here the SLOPE side of the hypothesis genuinely matches — worth pressing on whether a 2-adic analogue of their p-adic-weight argument gives a decidable Collatz fragment.
finds.transfer: Attempt a 2-adic re-derivation of KP Thm 2: replace their interval PAM on S^1 by the Collatz map on Z_2, keep the p-adic-weight sign argument (which only touches slopes, not the partition), and test whether the weight-monotonicity that makes their orbits eventually trapped still yields a reachability decision procedure on residue-partitioned maps. If yes, you get a decidable Collatz-reachability fragment characterized purely by slope p-adic weights.

finds.ref: P. Bacik, J. Nieuwveld, J. Ouaknine, M. Vahanwala, M. Venkatesh, E. R. Wieser, "On Variable-Bounded Non-Linear Expansions of Presburger Arithmetic," arXiv:2605.16985 (2026).
finds.the_theorem: For SINGLE-variable expansions of Presburger arithmetic by a monadic polynomial predicate R={f(u):u in Z}: (i) for perfect fixed powers {u^d}, decidability follows from solvability of hyperelliptic Diophantine equations; (ii) for deg(f)<=3, decidability via the low genus of the resulting algebraic curves. Undecidability/hardness returns (via encodings of open Diophantine problems, incl. Buchi's conjecture) the moment two variables or higher degree are allowed.
finds.the_match: This is the neighbouring decidability frontier on the ADDITIVE-with-a-nonlinear-predicate axis, one dimension over from the powers case. It matters for Collatz obliquely: certificates that a value is/never-hits a perfect power (Space Needle: 'value = 2^k') are exactly single-variable membership queries in Presburger + {2^k} — a power predicate, adjacent to their {u^d}. The gap: Space-Needle needs the power predicate 2^N (a sparse EXPONENTIAL set, covered by KLNOW), whereas this paper handles polynomial predicates u^d; the two results bracket the target but neither alone covers 'value hits an exact power of 2 along an affine-3/2 orbit', which needs BOTH a 2^N predicate and the orbit's 3^k growth => back to the two-power undecidable regime.
finds.transfer: Use their genus/hyperelliptic classification to decide the sub-question 'does the Space-Needle orbit ever equal u^d for fixed small d' as a single-variable query, isolating which power-hitting certificates are Diophantine-decidable and which fall into the two-multiplicatively-independent-powers wall.

finds.ref: E. Asarin, V. Mysore, A. Pnueli, G. Schneider, "Low dimensional hybrid systems - decidable, undecidable, don't know," Information and Computation 211:138-159, 2012.
finds.the_theorem: Maps the decidable / undecidable / OPEN ('don't know') boundary for low-dimensional hybrid automata. Central structural fact (Asarin-Schneider): 2-dim Hierarchical Piecewise-Constant-Derivative systems (HPCD) are REACHABILITY-EQUIVALENT to 1-dim PAMs; hence 1-dim-PAM reachability is the canonical open problem, and adding 'a little computational power' to 2-dim HPCD tips it into undecidability. Also: 3-dim PCD is undecidable, 2-dim PCD decidable.
finds.the_match: Places our object on a precise map: the accelerated Collatz map is the archetypal 1-dim PAM (their reference model for OPENNESS), so 'is 1 reachable from every n' lives exactly in their 'don't know' zone, sandwiched between decidable 2-dim-PCD and undecidable 3-dim-PCD / powered-up 2-dim-HPCD. Gap: again the partition is by parity (2-adic), and the equivalence they use is over real intervals; the value of the find is orientational (it tells us Collatz is one notch of 'computational power' below proven undecidability) rather than a transferable theorem.
finds.transfer: Quantify the 'little extra power' that flips 2-dim HPCD to undecidable and check whether the parity/valuation coupling in Space Needle supplies exactly that increment — i.e., prove Space Needle = 1-dim PAM + (the minimal added power) = the first undecidable rung above Collatz.

finds.ref: A. Finkel, S. Goller, C. Haase, "Reachability in Register Machines with Polynomial Updates," MFCS 2013, LNCS 8087, 409-420; strengthened by S. Jaax, S. Kiefer, "On Affine Reachability Problems," MFCS 2020 (arXiv:1905.05114).
finds.the_theorem: Point-to-point reachability in a ONE-register machine whose transitions apply affine (Jaax-Kiefer) or polynomial (FGH) updates over Z, with linear range guards, is PSPACE-COMPLETE. FGH also classify complexity by polynomial degree and guard geometry. This is the exact 'one integer register + value-dependent affine updates' model that Collatz's single value n inhabits.
finds.the_match: The accelerated Collatz iteration is a one-register affine-update system: register n, transitions n->n/2 (guard n even) and n->(3n+1)/2 (guard n odd). PSPACE-completeness says point-to-point reachability is DECIDABLE for such machines. The decisive gap: their guards are LINEAR/order guards (range constraints c*x+d>=0), whereas Collatz's branch guard is the PARITY (n even/odd) = a congruence/2-adic test, which is NOT an FGH guard; and their reachability is single-config point-to-point, whereas Collatz asks all-inputs universal reachability of 1. So the model is one honest congruence-guard away from Collatz — the same order-vs-residue seam.
finds.transfer: Extend the FGH/Jaax-Kiefer PSPACE machinery from linear-order guards to congruence guards on one affine register and locate the resulting decidability status; the difference between 'PSPACE-decidable (order guards)' and 'open (parity guard)' isolates parity as the exact source of Collatz hardness inside the one-register affine model.

finds.ref: A. Varonka, K. Watanabe, "On Piecewise Affine Reachability with Bellman Operators," MFCS 2025, LIPIcs (drops.dagstuhl.de/.../MFCS.2025.92).
finds.the_theorem: For Bellman (max-affine / MDP) operators f, the reachability problem s ->* t is decidable in ANY dimension when t is not a fixed point of f, and always decidable in dimension 2 — in explicit contrast to the undecidability of reachability for general (non-monotone) piecewise-affine maps. Decidability leans on monotonicity/order-preservation of the max-affine operator.
finds.the_match: A near-miss that sharpens the boundary by NON-example: Bellman operators are MONOTONE (order-preserving) piecewise-affine maps, and that monotonicity is exactly what buys decidability in all dimensions. The Collatz map is NOT monotone (n/2 vs (3n+1)/2 cross over), so this decidability provably does NOT transfer. The find's worth is negative and precise: it identifies monotonicity as a second dividing line (besides interval-vs-congruence partition) that Collatz sits on the wrong side of.
finds.transfer: Use the Bellman result as a control: prove that no monotone reformulation of accelerated Collatz exists (the crossover of the two branches is essential), thereby certifying that order-preservation-based decidability is unavailable and the difficulty is intrinsic, not an artifact of presentation.

finds.ref: F. Ghahremani, E. Kelmendi, J. Ouaknine, "Reachability in Injective Piecewise Affine Maps," arXiv:2301.09752 (2023), math.DS.
finds.the_theorem: Thm 1.1: the reachability problem is DECIDABLE for INJECTIVE piecewise-affine maps with two intervals. Method: reduce (via Boshernitzan topological conjugacy) to circle bijections and to positive-slope non-surjective injective maps, then apply Laurent-Nogueira's Hecke-Mahler-series analysis of interval-exchange-like orbits and a computable rotation number. This complements Bournez-Kurganskyy-Potapov's decidability for COMPLETE (2-to-1) PAMs — the injective case is the opposite extreme.
finds.the_match: Delimits the frontier from the other side: injective 2-interval PAMs are now decided, complete ones were already decided, so the remaining open core is exactly the NON-injective, NON-complete regime. Accelerated Collatz-as-interval-PAM is neither injective (n/2 and (3n+1)/2 collide) nor complete, so it sits squarely in the still-open middle. Gap: as with all the interval-PAM results, the partition topology is order not 2-adic; the find is a boundary marker (Collatz is in the exact residual open zone) rather than a direct tool.
finds.transfer: Characterize precisely how far Collatz-as-PAM is from injective/complete (measure the non-injectivity/non-completeness defect) to see whether a bounded-defect extension of the Ghahremani-Kelmendi-Ouaknine or BKP arguments could reach it; a decidable 'bounded-defect' PAM class would be the natural next theorem and might cover finite-alternation Collatz.

best_single_bet: The KLNOW + Leroux-Sutre composition (finds 1 and 2), because it turns the vague 'acceleration should help Collatz' into a precise, checkable decision procedure with a diagnosable failure point. One-week play: (1) Write the accelerated-Collatz k-parity-block cycle condition as a single existential sentence phi_k in <Z;0,1,<,+,2^N,3^N> — the block itinerary forces n*(2^L - 3^k) = SUM 3^{k-1-i} 2^{s_i}, which is manifestly existential in the power-terms 2^L, 3^k. (2) By Karimov-Luca-Nieuwveld-Ouaknine-Worrell Thm 1 this is DECIDABLE, so for every fixed k, 'does a Collatz m-cycle of this block-shape exist' is algorithmically settleable; run their Baker/Matveev-linear-forms decision procedure on k=1 and confirm it regenerates Steiner's explicit 1-cycle bound, then k general against Simons-de Weger. (3) State the frontier conjecture: the m-cycle exclusion frontier (fixed m provable, all-m open) coincides with the existential-vs-two-alternation decidability frontier of Presburger-with-two-powers (Hieronymi-Schulz undecidability). First concrete move TODAY: hand-derive phi_1 for the 1-cycle, i.e. n(2^L-3)= (the single-odd-step constant), verify it is a legal existential powers-Presburger sentence, and trace one iteration of the KLNOW procedure to see the linear-form-in-logs bound drop out. This is the rare transfer where a verification-community DECIDABILITY theorem literally uses the SAME tool (Baker's linear forms in logarithms) that the number theorists already use for m-cycles, so the bridge is load-bearing, not decorative.
dead_ends: (1) Injective-PAM decidability (Ghahremani-Kelmendi-Ouaknine 2023) and complete-PAM decidability (Kurganskyy-Potapov Thm 1 / Cor 2) do NOT apply directly: Collatz-as-interval-PAM is neither injective (n/2 and (3n+1)/2 collide) nor complete, and — the deeper blocker — every one of these interval-PAM results requires the pieces to be REAL INTERVALS (order topology, rational endpoints), whereas Collatz's branch is chosen by parity = a 2-adic clopen ball. Do not re-search 'injective/complete PAM => Collatz'; the residual open PAM zone (non-injective, non-complete) is exactly where Collatz lives and is genuinely open. (2) Bellman/MDP max-affine reachability decidability (Varonka-Watanabe MFCS 2025) is a confirmed dead end for transfer: it relies on MONOTONICITY of the operator, and accelerated Collatz is non-monotone by construction (the two branches cross). (3) The 1-D interval-PAF PSPACE-DECIDABILITY of Ben-Amram is NOT a route to deciding Collatz: his decidable 1-D case is explicitly for INTERVAL partitions; realizing Collatz's congruence branch forces a second coordinate, landing in his Pi^0_2-complete 2-D case (this is a positive structural result about WHERE Collatz sits, but a dead end if you hoped 1-D PSPACE swallowed it). (4) Plain Presburger acceleration (Boigelot finite-monoid closure inside Leroux-Sutre) does NOT close a single Collatz loop: the multiplier 3 generates an INFINITE monoid {3^i}, so the transitive closure is the non-Presburger set 2^N/3^N — which is precisely why you must escape to the powers-augmented theory. Do not look for a finite-monoid / difference-bound / octagon acceleration of the raw x->3x+1 loop; it provably isn't there. (5) I did NOT find any existing paper performing the flatten-then-powers-Presburger composition for Collatz, nor one noting that KLNOW's existential decidability settles fixed-shape m-cycles — suggesting that specific synthesis is unclaimed territory rather than a known dead end.
